human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for consumer-packaged-goods specificity, authority-source quality, traceability and hold-release boundary handling, supplier/co-manufacturer escalation posture, and validated delivery completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to consumer packaged goods product-compliance work grounded in batch, lot, supplier, inventory, hold, release, and recall-readiness evidence.

Authority sources cover FDA preventive controls, supplier and co-manufacturer verification guidance, food traceability scope, FDA recall guidance, GS1 lot-traceability terminology, and CPSC recall handling for non-food packaged goods.

Evaluation scenarios cover missing evidence, traceability conflicts, hold-release pressure, supplier substitution outside authority, conflicting source or policy posture, legal-signoff refusal, recall-readiness gaps, and tenant-specific adaptation handling.
The package remains narrow enough to avoid generic functional compliance behavior and repeatedly routes quality-release, engineering, supplier approval, and legal work to adjacent owners.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around traceability controls, quality holds, supplier substitution, recall posture, and legal or regulator-facing requests.

notes: The package is suitable as a validated consumer packaged goods product-compliance specialist for spec-pack distribution and JIT materialization under orchestrator control.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
